title: Outcome Receipts manifest
description: The receipts.json manifest exported alongside a report. Every figure carries a receipt: the exact query, the slice it was computed over, the value, and how the slice hash was produced. The schema is versioned; see docs/decisions/0005-receipt-canonicalization-and-schema.md.

schema_version.description: The manifest schema version. Consumers check this before re-deriving fields.

canonicalization.description: The rule set that turns a slice into the hashed bytes. Bumped when those rules change.

receipts.description: One entry per figure, sorted by metric_id.

metric_id.description: The stable identifier of the metric.

value.description: The numeric value the figure asserts.

display.description: The canonical rendering the grounding gate matches on.

definition.description: The plain-language definition of what the figure counts.

caveat.description: An optional qualifying note that travels with the figure and its receipt.

indicator.description: The optional logic-model indicator this figure measures.

data_source.description: The optional source system for the indicator data.

collection_frequency.description: The optional cadence at which the indicator data is collected.

value_sql.description: The query that produced the scalar value.

row_count.description: The number of rows in the slice.

slice_hash.description: The hex digest over the canonicalized slice; the all-zero sentinel marks an empty slice.

column_names.description: The slice's column names, in query order. Folded into the slice hash under canonicalization v1.

kind.description: Whether the figure is an activity count (output) or a change in condition (outcome).

computed_at.description: ISO timestamp from the injected clock. Not checked by verify.

provenance.description: Optional machine-readable attestation that no number was written by a model and the gate bound every number before export.

artifacts.description: Optional map of bundle-relative artifact path (report.md, trace.html, chart SVGs) to the sha256 hex digest of its bytes, recorded at export so `verify --bundle` can check that sibling files were not swapped. See docs/decisions/0006-whole-bundle-verification.md.
